# Bloks Reviews

### The community submitted 15 reviews to tell us what they like about Bloks, what Bloks can do better, and more. [...] Report

 6 views [...] Report

 3 views

•1 review

Started using this early and then it became often. There is a nuance to building "yet another note taking app" and where Bloks succeeds is in the stuff it doesn't try to do. Focused on gathering the information that you need at the time you need it -- all the RIGHT information -- Bloks has become my brain with very little setup or maintenance effort...just the way I like it ;). Thanks for building this with thought folks.

results.title: The best AI notetakers to use in 2026 | Product Hunt
results.content:
+ End-to-end encryption (data encrypted on device before upload).
  + On‑device AI processing for speech-to-text when possible (minimizes cloud exposure).
  + User‑controlled access and sharing so you decide who can see recordings.
  + Local storage with optional sync — audio kept on your device and synced across your devices when online.

  Those features improve privacy, but always check the app’s privacy policy, encryption details, and local consent laws before using discreet background recording.

results.title: ConnectMachine: A private AI agent that manages your network and connections | Product Hunt
results.content:
ConnectMachine is an AI Agent for Contact Management and creates smart digital business cards designed with a luxury mindset for the elites who prefer privacy and silent networking over noise.  
  
Features  
💳 Create multiple digital business cards for business, family, or events  
🤖 AI agent to query your contact network using voice or text  
🧠 Intelligent AI agent that recalls who you met, when, where, and why  
📅 Personal AI agent to manage your calendar, preferences, and interactions  
✨ AI powered contact enrichment using publicly available context  
📇 Scan physical business cards, QR codes, or share contacts via AirDrop  
📲 Google Wallet integration for instant, lock screen exchanges  
🤝 Discreet connections with nearby or inner circle members via silent reach outs [...] Report

Rohan Chaubey

Hunter

Many congratulations to the ConnectMachine team on the launch. Excited to hunt it today!

Dunbar’s number suggests humans can maintain around 150 meaningful relationships, which makes it hard to actively remember and nurture a large network on their own.

Products like ConnectMachine feel exciting because they use AI to extend those limits in practice by enriching contacts, surfacing relevant people and helping manage relationships more intelligently.

The AI agent and feature set look very compelling, and after seeing ConnectMachine, it felt like the exact kind of product I had previously imagined building.

Many congratulations again, genuinely rooting for your success! :)

Report

Giodio Mitaart [...] Custom Virtual Cards → Create multiple digital identities and share only what you choose, with whom you choose.
 Effortless Exchange → Scan business cards, QR codes, or share contacts via AirDrop. Every scan is personalized and ephemeral.
 Lock Screen Priority via Apple Wallet → Keep your ConnectMachine card live and ready for instant exchange.
 Silent Reachouts → Send discreet signals to nearby or inner-circle members without exposure or noise.
 Silent Alerts Only → No push notifications and no chaos. Just calm, meaningful connection.
 Zero APIs & Export-Only Contacts → Your network is private by design with no third-party integrations or external sharing.

results.title: Steam: Powerful PC gaming made easy | Product Hunt
results.content:
#### vs Alternatives

I seriously considered just building my own custom mini-ITX living room PC, or just giving up on PC gaming in the living room and buying a PlayStation 5. I ultimately chose the Steam Machine because building a DIY rig means dealing with Windows updates, driver conflicts, and using a mouse/keyboard on the couch just to launch games. On the flip side, moving to a traditional console meant abandoning my massive backlog of Steam games. The Steam Machine was the only option that gave me the seamless "couch experience" of a PS5 while letting me keep my

Gaming services by IFTTT
Chelsea C.

#### Alternatives Considered [...] However, outlets like Digital Foundry really highlighted where this machine shines: the software. The consensus across the board is that Valve has nailed the "console-like" experience with SteamOS. You are paying a premium, but you're paying for a frictionless, plug-and-play living room PC. If you just want to turn it on, grab a controller, and play your Steam library without troubleshooting Windows drivers or game launchers, it absolutely delivers.

#### What needs improvement

Upgradability: As Gamers Nexus pointed out, the smaller form factor makes it notoriously difficult to upgrade internal components down the line compared to a standard PC.

Pricing Tiers: The $1,049 starting point for 512GB is already tough, but the jump to $1,349 just to get 2TB of storage feels punishing. [...] Non-Steam Launchers: Out of the box, SteamOS is amazing for Steam games, but getting games from Epic Games Store, GOG, or Xbox Game Pass running still requires jumping through Linux workarounds. A more native way to install third-party storefronts would be a huge improvement.

#### vs Alternatives
